title: Second Suzuki Safari Town Festival to rock Oudtshoorn
description: BUSINESS NEWS - There's a party going...
bodyText:
Advertorial

BUSINESS NEWS - There's a party going down and everyone is invited! Following the success of the inaugural event in 2023, Suzuki Auto South Africa is thrilled to announce that the second Suzuki Safari Town Festival will be happening in Oudtshoorn on 27 September.


> Forming part of the broader Jimny Gathering weekend, this fun-filled celebration of life, freedom and the unmistakable Suzuki spirit will rock the historic Oudtshoorn Recreation Ground with an incredible line-up of homegrown musical talent.


Music lovers can expect to revel in energetic live performances from headlining acts such as Jeremy Loops, Spoegwolf, Karen Zoid and Gqeberha rap sensation Early B, just to name a few.

"The first Suzuki Safari Town Festival exceeded all our expectations, the energy, the turnout, the sense of community. It set the tone for something truly special," says Henk van der Schyf, the event's organiser. "For 2025, we've taken everything people loved and turned it up a notch. More music, more adventure and more ways to celebrate the spirit of Suzuki and the beautiful Karoo town of Oudtshoorn. We can't wait to welcome everybody back."

Not just a music festivalWhile live music is most certainly the main attraction, Suzuki and the event organisers have arranged for a large selection of food and refreshment vendors to add an extra level of enjoyment to the proceedings.

On top of this, festival-goers can delight in various craft bars, a large entertainment area for kids and opportunities throughout the day to meet and mingle with the performing artists, including comedian Schalk Bezuidenhout, who returns as the event's official MC, bringing his signature wit, charm and humour.

Though the Jimny Gathering is tailored for Suzuki Jimny owners and 4x4 enthusiasts, with four days of trails and adventure set to play out in the nearby Outeniqua Mountains, the Suzuki Safari Town Festival is open to the public. Everyone is welcome, whether you drive a Jimny or simply want to enjoy great music and lots of gees under the Karoo sun.


> "Suzuki is a brand for all South Africans. While we have some exciting activities planned specifically for Jimny owners, we also wanted to make sure everyone visiting Oudtshoorn for the weekend can join in the fun," explains Brendon Carpenter, brand marketing manager at Suzuki Auto South Africa.


"Our first Suzuki Safari Town Festival in Clarens was filled with good vibes and plenty of lekker people, and we're expecting this edition to be even better. We're looking forward to seeing you at the main stage!"

Tickets range from R60 to R250 and will be available at www.safaritown.co.za.

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
